course.title: JavaScript Basics
course.description: Learn the fundamentals of JavaScript, the programming language of the web.

chapter.title: Functions
chapter.description: Learn how to write reusable blocks of code using functions.

lessons.title: Introduction to Functions
objectives: Define and call a simple function
Understand the concept of reusable code
lessons.explanation: A JavaScript function is a block of code designed to perform a particular task. A function is executed when 'something' invokes it (calls it).

exercises.statement: Define a function named `sayHi` that prints 'Hi there!' to the console. Then call the function.
exercises.starter_code:
// Define your function below


// Call your function below
exercises.hint: Use function sayHi() { console.log('Hi there!'); } and then write sayHi(); to call it.

lessons.title: Function Parameters and Returns
objectives: Pass data into functions using parameters
lessons.explanation: Functions can take inputs called parameters, and they can send data back to where they were called using the `return` keyword.

exercises.statement: Write a function named `multiply` that takes two parameters, multiplies them together, and returns the result. Then call it with the numbers 5 and 6, and print the result.
exercises.starter_code:
// Write your function below


// Call the function and print the result
exercises.hint: The function should use the * operator and the return keyword.
